response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput systems experience (low-latency services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and strong TypeScript — all directly relevant. However, the must-have 'strong Python' is absent from both the skills list and every role described, the distributed-systems work was primarily in DLang and is several years old, and there is little concrete evidence of directing technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination, or operational/on-call ownership at staff scale. Worth a conversation if Python can be verified, but not a clear fit on paper.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Current AI-native exposure: builds MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching/mentoring track record
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume — a stated must-have skill
Distributed-systems depth is DLang-based and dates to 2018–2020, not recent
Little demonstrated technical-strategy leadership, stakeholder management, or on-call/operational ownership
